title: Present subjunctive — regular formation
objectives: Form the present subjunctive of any regular verb
Understand that the subjunctive is a MOOD, not a tense

vocab.es: hable / hables / hable / hablemos / habléis / hablen
coma / comas / coma / comamos / comáis / coman
viva / vivas / viva / vivamos / viváis / vivan

grammar_notes: Take the YO form of the present indicative, drop the -o, and add 'opposite-vowel' endings.
-AR verbs use E-endings: -e, -es, -e, -emos, -éis, -en. -ER and -IR verbs use A-endings: -a, -as, -a, -amos, -áis, -an.

The subjunctive marks UNREAL, WANTED, DOUBTED, EMOTIONAL situations — not facts. It's a mood that doesn't exist in English as a separate form, but exists in Russian (сослагательное наклонение with 'бы' covers some of it).
Russian intuition: 'Хочу, чтобы он пришёл' uses subjunctive-like 'пришёл' after 'чтобы'. Spanish: 'Quiero que venga' — same instinct.
example_phrases: Quiero que hables más alto. (I want you to speak louder.)
